query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "macaron-a2ui: a model for generative ui in personal agents" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) by measuring the relevance and utility of code as a user interface for end-user programming in the context of generative AI, which is a core variable and domain of the user's question regarding generative UI models. It also aligns with criterion (f) as it addresses the canonical alternative-vocabulary cluster (end-user programming, code relevance) for the mechanism of how LLMs interact with users to create software interfaces.
